issue.title: Bug: Dependency graph version dropdown uses string sort instead of semver — v0.2.9 appears after v0.2.15

successCriteria.description: DependencyGraph.tsx no longer references localeCompare for version ordering
Active-status releases stay pinned to top of dropdown
Backend semver order from cmp_semver (commands.rs:251) is preserved through to dropdown render
pnpm build passes from repo root (uses tsc -b, stricter than --noEmit)

phases.title: Remove broken localeCompare, trust backend semver sort
phases.content:
Edit `apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/DependencyGraph.tsx:26-32`. The Rust backend's `load_tiki_releases` already returns releases sorted descending by semver via `cmp_semver` (`apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:251`). The frontend `sortedReleases` useMemo then calls `b.version.localeCompare(a.version)`, which is lexicographic and undoes the correct order ('v0.2.9' > 'v0.2.15' under string compare).

Fix (Option A from the issue body): replace the `return b.version.localeCompare(a.version)` line with `return 0`. Keep the active-first promotion block above it intact. `Array.prototype.sort` is stable in modern engines (V8, JavaScriptCore), so a `return 0` comparator preserves the backend's existing semver order while the active-first comparison still pulls active releases to the top.

This matches the resolution pattern used for issue #120 (the analogous sidebar ReleasesSection bug, already shipped 2026-02-20). #148 is the second-consumer regression — DependencyGraph.tsx was added later as part of #92 and didn't inherit the fix.
verification: Visual check: the active-first promotion `if (a.status === 'active' && ...)` block is still present in DependencyGraph.tsx:26-32
`pnpm build` passes from repo root with no new errors

Manual: launch desktop app, open Dependency Graph view, confirm dropdown shows v0.5.1 (active) first, then v0.2.15 → v0.2.14 → ... → v0.2.5 in correct descending order (not the broken v0.2.9, v0.2.8, ... v0.2.15 order)
